hypothesis:
Based on the paper's findings that CAST enables property-conditional molecular generation through activation steering but currently focuses only on scalar continuous properties (QED and LogP), a novel hypothesis extending beyond this work would be:

**Hypothesis:** Incorporating graph-structured concept embeddings into the CAST framework will enable simultaneous control over both scalar molecular properties and discrete substructural constraints (e.g., specific functional groups or scaffold motifs), resulting in improved scaffold diversity and functional group retention compared to scalar-only CAST while maintaining equivalent or better property alignment accuracy.
